articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Socceroos slump to frustrating Venezuela loss
article.description: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic will look to quickly move on from a frustrating 1-0 defeat to Venezuela and hope his younger, inexperienced players learn from it too.
article.linkText: Socceroos slump to frustrating Venezuela loss

images.name: AUSvVEN live [1296x729]
images.credit: Tim Warner/Getty Images for Soccer Australia

peers.name: AUSvVEN live [1296x1296]
peers.credit: Tim Warner/Getty Images for Soccer Australia

peers.name: AUSvVEN live [1296x729]
peers.credit: Tim Warner/Getty Images for Soccer Australia

peers.name: AUSvVEN live [1296x864]
peers.credit: Tim Warner/Getty Images for Soccer Australia

peers.name: AUSvVEN live [1296x518]
peers.credit: Tim Warner/Getty Images for Soccer Australia

article.story:
Socceroos coach Tony Popovic will look to quickly move on from a frustrating 1-0 defeat to Venezuela and hope his younger, inexperienced players learn from it too.

Jesús Ramírez's header in the 38th minute proved the difference against an experimental Australian team at Shell Energy Stadium in Houston.

Few players, bar "fantastic" debutant goalkeeper Patrick Beach, impressed in an uncharacteristically flat performance where Australia lacked drive, cohesion and potency and had just 34% of possession.

In their second consecutive defeat, Australia also lost defender Jason Geria to a left hamstring injury in the 42nd minute.

It will give Popovic plenty to think about with just three more friendlies before picking his squad for next year's World Cup finals, starting with Wednesday's clash with world No.13 Colombia in New York.

"Great challenge on Tuesday (local time), and we know we have to be better, but that's part of what we're doing here and part of the process," Popovic said.

With Jordy Bos and Aziz Behich among a lengthy injury list, Popovic turned to Craig Goodwin at left wingback.

The experiment didn't pay off, with Goodwin exposed for Venezuela's goal then hooked for Callum Elder at half-time.

Popovic otherwise fielded a relatively inexperienced team against a similarly understrength Venezuela, who missed World Cup qualification.

"There's a lot we have to assess, but in the end we lost, we're disappointed we lost the game," Popovic said, conceding Australia struggled physically.

"We gave a lot of players debuts, and we gave a lot of players an opportunity, some maybe in different positions.

"So it was a little bit difficult for us in that respect, and maybe players not quite understanding their roles -- which is quite difficult in a couple of days -- and making a debut et cetera.

"But we fought hard, we worked hard. We were probably a little bit too open.

"Venezuela technically were very good and were able to get into our box and obviously scored a goal, but it was a little bit too easy from our part."

Beach started ahead of Maty Ryan and Paul Izzo while Melbourne City defender Kai Trewin and Sydney FC midfielder Paul Okon-Engstler, son of Socceroos assistant Paul Okon, also started on debut.

Al Hassan Toure was the fourth debutant in the 78th minute, after brother Mohamed had already been substituted, becoming Popovic's 12th debutant in 13 games.

Mohamed Toure spurned two great chances in the opening six minutes, before Venezuela got on top.

Beach made an extraordinary reflex save to bat away Jesus Ramirez's goal-bound header in the 17th minute.

For the goal, Goodwin was caught high up the pitch and Ender Echenique was released down the right wing, cut inside Trewin then squared the ball for Ramirez to tap home.

"Really, it's a soft goal for us to give away," Popovic said.

"But maybe the contribution of that is having a debutant at left centreback, Craig Goodwin at left wing-back.

"... But okay, it was a lot of learnings for us, and we move on."

A minute into the second half, Beach denied Ramirez with a diving save.

Riley McGree and Jackson Irvine returned from injury in the second half and the substitutes lifted Australia.

Al Hassan Toure forced an excellent Contreras one-handed save in the 86th minute, but it proved too little too late.
